sections: When Long-Term Storage Is the Right Choice

Some belongings need a safe home for longer than a few weeks, and that is exactly the situation our Sussex storage facility is built for. As a family-run removals & storage company based at Doryln House on London Road in Ashington near Pulborough, we have looked after furniture and possessions for customers across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent since 2016 — often for many months at a time. Long-term storage tends to suit a particular set of life events rather than a quick gap between two homes, and recognising which one you are in helps you store the right way from day one.

The most common reasons our customers choose extended storage are remarkably consistent, and each one shapes how we recommend you pack and protect your goods. If any of the following describe your situation, a containerised long-term arrangement is almost always the most sensible and cost-effective route:

  • Working or living abroad for a contract, posting or sabbatical and keeping a UK furniture base to return to
  • An extended renovation, loft conversion or full refurbishment where rooms need clearing for months
  • Long-haul travelling, a career break or a stint of house-sitting with no fixed address
  • Inheriting furniture you are not ready to part with but cannot fit into your current home
  • Downsizing permanently and holding pieces back for family, a future move or sentimental reasons

Because every one of these is measured in months rather than days, the priorities shift. Accessibility matters less than security, climate and the way each item is wrapped for the long haul — and that is where our hands-on experience genuinely earns its keep.
How Containerised Storage Protects Your Furniture for the Long Haul

Our storage is containerised, which is the single biggest reason it outperforms a typical self-storage unit for anything kept over a long period. Rather than carting your belongings down a corridor and stacking them in an open bay, our team loads them into a dedicated timber container that is then sealed and set into our clean, dry, secure warehouse. Each container is yours alone — nobody else’s goods share the same space, and once it is filled and closed it stays closed until you need it back. That sealed-and-stacked approach dramatically reduces handling, dust, knocks and the gradual wear that comes from items being shuffled around an open floor.

Inside the warehouse the environment stays consistently clean and dry, which is what genuinely matters when furniture is going to sit untouched for half a year or more. Damp is the enemy of long-term storage: it lifts veneers, marks upholstery and tarnishes anything metal. A stable, dry building combined with proper wrapping keeps your pieces in the same condition they went in. We have stored everything from family heirlooms and antique cabinets to full three- and four-bedroom households this way, and our standing as a LAPADA member reflects the care we take with valuable and fragile items in particular. If you want to picture exactly how it works, our wider storage services overview walks through the containerised process in full.
Preparing & Protecting Your Belongings for Months in Store

The difference between furniture that emerges from twelve months’ storage looking pristine and furniture that comes out tired almost always comes down to the preparation that went in. For short stays you can get away with a quick wrap; for the long term, every item deserves proper treatment, because small problems have months to become big ones. Our crews prepare goods specifically for extended storage rather than a quick shuttle across town, and the details make all the difference.

Upholstered sofas and mattresses are covered to keep dust and moisture off the fabric, while polished and wooden surfaces are wrapped to prevent scuffs and protect any finish. We dismantle bed frames, tables and flat-pack units where it makes sense, keeping fixings bagged and labelled so reassembly on the way out is painless. Mirrors, glass and artwork are protected with care, and anything genuinely delicate is crated or padded to survive being stationary for a long stretch. For households where every box matters, pairing storage with our full packing service means professionally wrapped, correctly boxed contents from the outset — the way that survives months in store best.

  • Empty and defrost fridges and freezers thoroughly so no moisture or odour develops while they sit
  • Avoid sealing items in plastic for long periods, which can trap condensation against wood and fabric
  • Use sturdy boxes filled to the top so they stack squarely and never collapse under months of weight
  • Label every box by room and contents so retrieval later is quick and nothing has to be guessed at
  • Photograph valuable or antique pieces before they go in, giving you a clear record for your own peace of mind

This is preparation borne out of years of doing it across Sussex and the South East — not a checklist copied from elsewhere, but the routine our movers genuinely follow on every long-term job.
Flexible Rolling Terms That Suit a Changing Plan

One of the things people find most reassuring about long-term storage with us is that you are not locked into a rigid fixed term. We work on flexible rolling arrangements, which matters enormously when your plans are open-ended — a contract abroad that gets extended, a renovation that overruns, or a house search that takes longer than hoped. You keep your container for as long as you need it and bring it to a close when life settles, without penalty clauses forcing your hand. For customers heading overseas, this dovetails neatly with our international removals service, so the same family-run team can ship part of your home abroad while safely storing the rest at base.

That flexibility also means there is no awkward distinction to agonise over at the start. If you are genuinely unsure whether you need a few weeks or many months, our short-term storage option can quietly roll into a long-term stay without you having to move a thing — the container simply stays put and the arrangement carries on. Households moving for work and businesses holding stock or archives both benefit from the same principle, and our business and commercial storage follows exactly the same rolling, no-nonsense approach.
The Real Value of Long-Term Storage Versus Self-Storage

When you compare the headline rate of a self-storage unit against containerised storage, the unit can look cheaper at a glance — but for long stays the true picture is usually the other way around. With self-storage you pay for fixed-size floor space whether you fill it or not, you handle all the loading and unloading yourself, and you are responsible for hiring a van and crew at both ends. Across many months those costs and that effort add up, and your belongings sit in space that is rarely climate-considered and frequently over-handled.

Our long-term storage is priced on the volume you actually store, so you only pay for the container space your goods genuinely occupy. Our own trained movers collect, pack, load and stack everything, then return it to you when you are ready — no van hire, no heavy lifting, no weekend lost to a trolley and a lift. To get a realistic sense of how much space you will need before you commit, our storage calculator is the quickest starting point, and you can see how it all fits together on our pricing page. As a Checkatrade-verified and fully insured company carrying £10m liability cover, and one recommended by respected local agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, we like to think the value runs well beyond the monthly figure. You can read more about the family behind it on our about us page, or simply request a tailored storage quote and we will talk it through.
faqs: How do I access my belongings during a long-term storage period?

Because long-term storage is containerised, access is arranged in advance rather than on a walk-in basis — this is what keeps your goods sealed, secure and undisturbed for the whole period. If you need to retrieve a specific item or add to your container partway through, simply give us a little notice and we will arrange it for you. For long stays we always recommend labelling boxes clearly and noting where key items are packed, so anything you might want before the end can be reached quickly. Call us on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk to organise access.
Is my furniture safe in storage for a year or more?

Yes. Our warehouse in Ashington is clean, dry and secure, and your goods sit inside a sealed timber container that nobody else shares. That combination of a stable, dry environment and minimal handling is precisely what protects furniture, upholstery and antiques over long periods. We are fully insured with £10m liability cover and, as a LAPADA member, we take particular care with valuable and fragile pieces. With proper wrapping at the outset, items kept for a year or longer come out in the same condition they went in.
Can I switch from short-term to long-term storage if my plans change?

Absolutely — and you will not need to move anything to do it. We work on flexible rolling terms, so a stay that began as short-term storage can simply continue into a long-term arrangement while your container stays exactly where it is. This is ideal when a renovation overruns, a contract abroad is extended, or a house search takes longer than expected. There are no penalties for staying longer; you keep the container for as long as you need and close the arrangement when you are ready.
How should I prepare delicate or antique furniture for months in storage?

Delicate and antique pieces benefit from professional wrapping, padding and, where appropriate, crating before they go into store — this is something our crews do as standard. We cover upholstery against dust and moisture, protect polished surfaces, and avoid sealing wood in plastic, which can trap condensation over long periods. Dismantling larger items and bagging the fixings keeps everything safe and easy to reassemble later. Many customers combine storage with our full packing service so every item is correctly protected from the very start. For valuable pieces, photographing them beforehand gives you a clear record for added peace of mind.
